name: AWS Access Key ID
description: Identifies an AWS IAM account and, paired with its secret, grants programmatic access to your AWS resources.
remediation: Revoke the key in the AWS IAM console and load credentials at runtime via IAM roles or AWS Secrets Manager instead of .env.

name: AWS Secret Access Key
description: The private half of an AWS credential pair that signs API requests and can fully control your AWS account.
remediation: Rotate the secret immediately in AWS IAM and store it in AWS Secrets Manager or an IAM role, never in a committed file.

name: Stripe Live Secret Key
description: A live Stripe secret key that can create charges, issue refunds, and read customer payment data on your production account.
remediation: Roll the key in the Stripe Dashboard (Developers > API keys) and inject it through your host's secret manager at runtime.

name: Stripe Live Publishable Key
description: A live Stripe publishable key used in client code; while less sensitive, it confirms your live account and aids targeted abuse.
remediation: Keep publishable keys in frontend config only and confirm no secret key sits beside it in this file.

name: Stripe Test Secret Key
description: A Stripe test-mode secret key that only touches sandbox data but still should not be shared publicly.
remediation: Move it to a local-only .env that is git-ignored, and rotate it from the Stripe Dashboard if it was ever committed.

name: GitHub Personal Access Token
description: A GitHub token that can read or push to your repositories and act on your behalf across the GitHub API.
remediation: Revoke it under GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens and supply it via CI/CD encrypted secrets.

name: SendGrid API Key
description: A SendGrid API key that can send email as your domain and read mail activity, enabling spam or phishing from your account.
remediation: Delete the key in the SendGrid dashboard (Settings > API Keys) and store the replacement in your platform's secret store.

name: JWT Secret
description: The signing secret for your JSON Web Tokens; anyone holding it can forge valid auth tokens for any user.
remediation: Generate a new high-entropy secret, store it in a secret manager, and invalidate existing sessions after rotation.

name: Database URL with embedded password
description: A database connection string with an inline username and password granting direct read/write access to your data.
remediation: Rotate the database password and pass the connection string through an environment secret at deploy time, not a committed file.

name: Firebase API Key
description: A Google/Firebase API key that, without proper restrictions, can be abused to run up billing or access enabled APIs.
remediation: Restrict the key by referrer/IP in the Google Cloud Console and regenerate it if it was exposed publicly.

name: Twilio Auth Token
description: A Twilio auth token that lets an attacker send SMS, place calls, and incur charges on your Twilio account.
remediation: Rotate the token in the Twilio Console and reference it via a runtime secret rather than storing it in the repo.

name: OpenAI API Key
description: An OpenAI API key that can spend against your account quota and access your organization's model usage.
remediation: Revoke it on the OpenAI API keys page and inject the replacement through your deployment's secret manager.

name: Google OAuth Client Secret
description: A Google OAuth client secret used to authenticate your app during the OAuth flow; exposure enables app impersonation.
remediation: Reset the client secret in the Google Cloud Console (APIs & Services > Credentials) and keep it server-side only.

name: Slack Bot/App Token
description: A Slack bot or user token that can read messages and post into your workspace channels.
remediation: Revoke the token in your Slack app's OAuth settings and load it from an environment secret at runtime.

name: Generic High Entropy Secret
description: A variable whose name signals a credential and whose value is long enough to be a real secret.
remediation: Confirm whether this is a live secret; if so, rotate it and move it into a managed secret store outside version control.
